new_series_added.notes: SLOOS C&I Large/Medium Tightening; quarterly, interpolated to monthly; starts 1990Q2
SLOOS C&I Small Tightening; quarterly; starts 1990Q2
SLOOS Consumer Credit Cards Tightening; quarterly; starts 1996Q1
SLOOS Demand C&I Large/Medium; quarterly; starts 1991Q4 (correct ID -- original plan's SUBLPDCILMN does not exist on FRED)
Consumer credit outstanding; monthly; non-financial credit growth signal

new_series_fred_id_correction.reason: SUBLPDCILMN returns 'Bad Request. The series does not exist.' Verified via fredapi; DRSDCILM is the correct FRED ID for Net Percentage of Domestic Banks Reporting Stronger Demand for Commercial and Industrial Loans From Large and Middle-Market Firms, quarterly since 1991Q4.

anfci_notes: ANFCI was already in the FRED pull pre-B3 but had never been selected. After adding financial_ANFCI to the must-include list in ensemble_model.py, both the raw series and its MA6 transform now land in the selected-50.
must_include_survival_note: SLOOS_CI_LARGE_Z, SLOOS_COMPOSITE, and CREDIT_TIGHTEN_X_CURVE_INVERT are dropped by the 70%-non-null valid_cols gate because SLOOS starts 1990Q2 (~62% coverage in the 1970+ training frame). This is the expected graceful-degradation behavior. SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG and SLOOS_ACUTE_TIGHTENING (both binary, defaulting to 0 pre-1990, so ~100% coverage) survive selection. financial_ANFCI also passes the gate and is selected.
ebp_x_sloos_coverage_note: EBP_X_SLOOS has only 14 non-null rows because EBP_PROXY_Z is crippled by financial_BAMLH0A0HYM2 (HY OAS) and financial_BAMLC0A0CM (IG OAS) both only having 37 non-null rows in the cached FRED pull (2023-04 onward). This is a pre-existing data-access issue with those two series (same behavior in B1/B2), not introduced by B3. The EBP_X_SLOOS interaction is included in the engineered frame but cannot pass the valid_cols gate.

drop_ablation.notes: Biggest test-set impact is SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG (-17.2pp test PR-AUC, -16.8pp test AUC when zeroed), indicating it's load-bearing for recent-era (2015-2026) performance. CV PR-AUC movements are all within ~1pp (noise floor) -- no feature meets the 3pp CV PR-AUC drop-ablation KEEP gate. financial_ANFCI drop reduces test PR-AUC by 5.8pp. Full-history CV doesn't reward these features because their signal is concentrated in 1990+ (where SLOOS exists) and the recession base rate is low.

dot_com_peak_vs_at_freeze_baseline_8df6b.note: A1.75.2 identified Dot-com raw peak as the binding constraint for threshold crossing. +0.5pp does not materially move the needle.

baseline_8df6b9d_on_b3_data.scored_on_feature_frame: post-B3 (B3 features in df, but baseline only uses its original 50)
baseline_8df6b9d_on_b3_data.notes: Pinned baseline_8df6b9d pickles scored on the post-B3 feature frame's last 134 labeled rows. Isolates the code delta (B3 features added) from retraining variance: the baseline doesn't actually use B3 features, so this should be nearly identical to the at-freeze metrics unless the shared feature space subtly changed.

gate_checks.drop_ablation_ge_3pp_cv_loss: FAIL (largest CV drop-ablation loss is ~0.7pp on SLOOS_ACUTE_TIGHTENING)
gate_checks.recessions_detected_ge_3_of_4: PASS (3/4 in-scope, Dot-com still misses both variants)
gate_checks.no_inscope_regression_gt_10pct: BORDERLINE (COVID peak -0.1012 = ~10pp drop, COVID AUC -0.0789). COVID still detected (peak > threshold).
gate_checks.drop_ablation_test_pr_auc_impact: SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG delta_test_pr_auc=-0.1722; informational on test window (2014-2026).

verdict_reason: GFC lead time improves by +5.94 months (1.0->7.0 mo, clearly passing the +1mo KEEP gate), and S&L lead improves by +1.0mo (4.96->5.95). Two B3 engineered features earn top-20 selection (SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG at rank 2, SLOOS_ACUTE_TIGHTENING at rank 12), and the ANFCI family (which was in the frame pre-B3 but never selected) now picks up 2 slots. SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG drop-ablation shows -17.2pp test PR-AUC when zeroed (major test-window contributor). However, the A1.75.2 headline metric -- Dot-com calibrated peak -- barely moves (0.225 -> 0.230 vs no_credit; same 0.225 baseline_8df6b9d peak). In-scope CV PR-AUC regresses by 0.45pp (within noise, under the 1.5pp KEEP gate). COVID peak drops ~10pp (still detected; COVID is effectively a coincident signal in the backtest given -5/-6mo lead), and the CV-level drop-ablation shows no feature with >=3pp CV loss. Net: real GFC/S&L gains and modern-era credit signal validated, but the binding-constraint Dot-com peak did not move. Mark NEEDS-WORK -- credit block adds value on GFC lead time and feature diversity but the intended mechanism (lifting Dot-com raw signal via pre-2001 SLOOS tightening) did not materialize at the calibrated peak level. Suggest keeping B3 code in place on merge (the GFC lift and ANFCI activation are real) but do not promote to main until paired with a complementary Dot-com-raising intervention.
guidance_for_followups: B3 adds value on GFC lead time (+5.94mo) and S&L lead (+1mo), and injects ANFCI + SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG into selection with SLOOS_TIGHTENING_FLAG carrying major test-window weight. But it does not lift the Dot-com raw peak -- which A1.75.2 identified as the binding constraint for threshold crossing. Options for C-series follow-ups: (a) keep hybrid_no_b3_credit as production default given minor CV regression; (b) merge B3 code but document the NEEDS-WORK verdict and pair with a Dot-com-raising experiment (targeted post-1990 training, or Dot-com-specific feature engineering like 1999-2001 financial stress inflection metrics).
notes_for_validator: Running on experiment/B3-credit-supply rebased onto main at SHA aa493df. Reused B2 infrastructure (SUPPORTED_VARIANTS registry + --variant-output-dir plumbing). Added hybrid_no_b3_credit variant symmetric to hybrid_no_b2_labor. SUBLPDCILMN was corrected to DRSDCILM (the plan's series ID was invalid). Strict vintage search skipped per time budget. Ensemble CV PR-AUC uses the max-of-base-models convention from B1/B2.
